# About Quantum Dice
## Pioneers in probabilistic computing
Quantum Dice is bringing to market the first scalable probabilistic computing platform to help tackle some of the world's most critical problems: efficiently enabling the next generation of energy-based AI models and solving the complex optimisation problems increasingly encountered in modern industry.
[Discover our technology [3]](https://quantum-dice.com/technology)
## Mission
To deliver the scalable, purpose-built probabilistic computing hardware needed to unlock the true potential of probabilistic algorithms – making otherwise intractable computational problems solvable.
## Our story
Since spinning out from the University of Oxford's Department of Physics in 2020, Quantum Dice has commercialised its patented source of randomness, successfully deploying it in high-assurance cybersecurity applications. Building on this field-validated quantum technology and harnessing our high-speed, self-correcting quantum photonic sources of entropy, we have expanded into probabilistic computing – bringing a new approach to computation to market.
2020
Spin-out from University of Oxford's Department of Physics
2021
First quantum randomness source developed
2022
Deployment to high-assurance cybersecurity applications
2023-24
Development of on-chip high-speed source of randomness
2025
Expansion into probabilistic computing
## Ready to experience the future of compute?
[Try ORBIT [3]](https://cloud.yoursite.com)[Join our team [4]](https://quantum-dice.com/careers)

About Quantum Dice

Pioneers in probabilistic computing

Quantum Dice is bringing to market the first scalable probabilistic computing platform to help tackle some of the world's most critical problems: efficiently enabling the next generation of energy-based AI models and solving the complex optimisation problems increasingly encountered in modern industry.

Discover our technology [3]

Mission

To deliver the scalable, purpose-built probabilistic computing hardware needed to unlock the true potential of probabilistic algorithms – making otherwise intractable computational problems solvable.

Our story

Since spinning out from the University of Oxford's Department of Physics in 2020, Quantum Dice has commercialised its patented source of randomness, successfully deploying it in high-assurance cybersecurity applications. Building on this field-validated quantum technology and harnessing our high-speed, self-correcting quantum photonic sources of entropy, we have expanded into probabilistic computing – bringing a new approach to computation to market.

2020

Spin-out from University of Oxford's Department of Physics

2021

First quantum randomness source developed

2022

Deployment to high-assurance cybersecurity applications

2023-24

Development of on-chip high-speed source of randomness

2025

Expansion into probabilistic computing
